components.name: Bento Card
components.description: The primary container for tools and data.
components.html:
Subnet Info

Content goes here.
components.css: .ds-bento-card { background-color: rgba(24, 24, 27, 0.72); border: 1px solid #27272a; border-radius: 12px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 8px 32px -8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5); transition: all 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); color: #fafafa; font-family: 'Geist', sans-serif; } .ds-bento-card-hover:hover { transform: scale(1.005); box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.08), 0 8px 32px -8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), 0 0 60px -20px rgba(34, 211, 238, 0.06); }

narrative.overview: This system is built around a dark, glowing, developer-native atmosphere. It prioritizes clarity over decoration, treating the math and data as the hero. Layouts employ asymmetric precision rather than predictable, monotonous blocks. The aesthetic explicitly rejects generic "AI Slop" SaaS templates, the "card-nested-in-card" architecture, and heavy white backing sheets dropping onto flat gray canvas spaces.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: The primary accent (Electric Cyan) is used deliberately for interaction points and active states. It should not overwhelm the data.

dos: Do use content-driven, intentional layout grids rather than predictable blocks.
Do apply the Electric Cyan glow selectively to draw attention to active input or critical results.
donts: Don't use generic "AI Slop" SaaS templates.
Don't use redundant decorative icon tiles next to every heading.
Don't use giant, heavy white backing sheets dropping onto flat gray canvas spaces.
Don't use identical grid blocks that ignore content structure.
